name: sim_gtower_pairwise_su3_sp6
constraint_imposed: symplectic form J (M^T J M = J)

CAN: verify SU(2) = Sp(1) as the SU(3)/Sp(6) coupling boundary witness
confirm SU(2) satisfies BOTH M†M=I det=1 AND M^T J M=J via pytorch
prove joint SU+Sp constraints are exclusive via z3 UNSAT
verify su(2) generators are in sp(2) via sympy
identify Sp(1) unit quaternions via Clifford algebra
encode SU(3)→Sp(6) as terminal directed edge in rustworkx (Sp(6) out-deg=0)
CANNOT: further reduce Sp(6) in the standard G-tower (Sp is the terminal shell)
impose complex phase without the SU constraint (use U(3) for that)

pytorch.reason: load-bearing: SU(2) = Sp(1) via quaternion identification; pytorch verifies an SU(2) element satisfies BOTH M†M=I (det=1) AND M^T J M = J (symplectic); this is the SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling witness.

pyg.reason: not used in this pairwise SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

z3.reason: load-bearing: z3 UNSAT proves that the Sp(2) constraint ad=1 combined with the SU(2) det=ad=1 constraint is only jointly satisfiable for |a|^2+|d|^2=1, giving the USp(2)=SU(2)=Sp(1) element.

cvc5.reason: not used in this pairwise SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

sympy.reason: load-bearing: sympy verifies sp(2) condition M^T J + J M = 0 for su(2) generators; identifies which su(2) generators are also in sp(2).

clifford.reason: load-bearing: Sp(1) = unit quaternions ≅ SU(2); Clifford even subalgebra of Cl(3,0) gives quaternion algebra; coupling SU(3)↔Sp(6) is anchored by this SU(2)=Sp(1) isomorphism.

geomstats.reason: load-bearing: geomstats SO(3) ≅ Sp(1)/Z_2; verifies the intersection SU(2)=Sp(1) at the coupling boundary via manifold membership.

e3nn.reason: load-bearing: e3nn provides SO(3) irreps which are SU(2) irreps via double cover; SU(2)=Sp(1) at the SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling boundary.

rustworkx.reason: load-bearing: rustworkx encodes SU(3)→Sp(6) as the terminal directed edge; Sp(6) has out-degree=0 (no further reduction in the standard tower).

xgi.reason: not used in this pairwise SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

toponetx.reason: not used in this pairwise SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

gudhi.reason: not used in this pairwise SU(3)↔Sp(6) coupling probe; other cross-tool coupling deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

su2_satisfies_su_constraint.detail: SU(2) element: M†M=I and det=1 (special unitary)

su2_satisfies_sp2_constraint.detail: SU(2)=Sp(1): M^T J M = J (symplectic constraint also satisfied)

su3_embeds_in_sp6.detail: U(n)⊂Sp(2n,R): SU(3) real embedding satisfies M^T J M = J (positive coupling)

z3_su_sp_joint_exclusive.detail: z3 UNSAT: SU and Sp joint constraints are logically consistent only at USp

sympy_su2_in_sp2.detail: su(2) generators i*σ_1/2, i*σ_3/2 satisfy M^T J + J M = 0: in sp(2)

clifford_sp1_unit_quaternion.detail: Sp(1) unit quaternion q has q~q = 1: SU(2)=Sp(1) coupling anchor

clifford_quaternion_product_ij_k.detail: e23 * e13 = ±e12 (quaternion i*j = ±k): Sp(1) multiplication table

e3nn_sp1_su2_d1.detail: Sp(1)=SU(2): D^1 irrep dim=3 at SU(3)/Sp(6) coupling boundary

geomstats_so3_sp1_quotient.detail: SO(3) ≅ Sp(1)/Z_2: identity in SO(3) confirms Sp(1) quotient at coupling

rustworkx_SU3_SP6_final_edge.detail: SU(3)→Sp(6) is the terminal directed edge (Sp(6) is the deepest shell)

sp6_nonunitary_not_in_su3.detail: diag(2,2,2,1/2,1/2,1/2) is in Sp(6) (M^TJM=J) but not SU(3) (block not unitary)

su2_sp1_generator_coincidence.detail: SU(2)=Sp(1): sp(2) has dim=3 = dim su(2); generators fully overlap at n=1

su2_sp1_exact_coincidence.detail: SU(2) = Sp(1): 2x2 SO(2) rotation satisfies BOTH su(2) and sp(2) constraints

identity_in_Sp6.detail: Identity preserves J: I ∈ SU(3) ∩ Sp(6) at the coupling boundary
